title: G3: Vanishing of signature on boundaries (half lives, half dies) elementarily

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Claim to prove.** If $M^{4k}=\partial W^{4k+1}$ with $W$ compact oriented, then $\tau(M)=0$.

**Required argument (elementary).** By Poincaré–Lefschetz duality on $W$ and the long exact sequence of $(W,M)$, show the image $A=\mathrm{im}(H^{2k}(W;\mathbb{R})\to H^{2k}(M;\mathbb{R}))$ is a **maximal isotropic (Lagrangian) subspace** of half the dimension for the intersection form on $M$ (it is self-annihilating, and $\dim A=\tfrac12\dim H^{2k}(M)$). A nondegenerate symmetric form admitting a Lagrangian has signature 0.

**Also needed (companion):** Pontryagin numbers vanish on boundaries, so $\langle L,[\cdot]\rangle$ is likewise a cobordism invariant. This must be argued **without** the banned machinery — e.g. via Chern–Weil/Stokes ($p_i$ represented by curvature forms on a metric extended over $W$) or an explicit elementary argument. Note: it must be checked whether Chern–Weil counts as 'deep machinery' under the problem's ban (it bans heat kernels and index theory; Chern–Weil is arguably elementary, but this should be justified explicitly).
